twitter_thread: 97% of LLM AI systems are powered by just 3 Big Tech companies, with 0 public audits.
Fine-tuning LLMs with in-house data can boost performance by 35%, but requires 12+ weeks of training.
LLaMA base models cut costs by 42% compared to BERT, in a 10-model benchmark.
Ignoring prompt engineering can lead to 25% worse results, as seen in a 500-prompt study.
Named entity recognition errors drop by 18% when using spaCy with LLMs, in a 10k-sample test.
5% of LLM AI errors are due to flawed data, not the model itself, according to a 20-model review.
What's the most surprising error you've seen in an LLM AI system - and how did you fix it? #llm #ai
pack.linkedin: Big Tech's LLM AI dominance raises questions about transparency, with 97% of systems controlled by just 3 companies. Fine-tuning with in-house data can improve performance by 35%, but requires significant training time. LLaMA base models offer 42% cost savings, while prompt engineering and named entity recognition with spaCy can optimize results. However, 5% of errors stem from flawed data, not the model. What's your take on the current state of LLM AI - are we prioritizing accountability?
